registryPage.checkSlug: Check that the slug exists in web/config/notebooks.json.

ekopfo.description: The provided documents describe a patient medical records management system, focusing on processes related to functional status and disability assessment.
ekopfo.commentary: The documents detail the roles and responsibilities of various medical professionals, including attending physicians, expert team leaders, expert team members, and medical registrars. The sources cover aspects such as case submission, editing and withdrawal, protocol and extract formation, as well as case and protocol statuses reflecting their progress in the system. Additionally, the instructions explain functions related to personal data, journal keeping, expert team schedule management, and conflict of interest resolution, providing a comprehensive description of digital document workflow in a medical institution.

userSupportText.intro: If you have questions or need assistance, we offer the following support channels:

userSupportText.faqIntro: We also have ready links for answers to the most common questions:
faqItems: How to report errors to tech support for the EKOPFO IT system (step by step)
How to create an employee electronic signature linked to a legal entity
userSupportText.instructionsText: Official User Instructions for the EKOPFO system are located here

endoprosthesis.description: The provided documents describe an electronic system for managing the queue for free endoprosthetics in Ukraine.
endoprosthesis.commentary: The documents detail the system's purpose, user roles (such as representatives of health departments, medical institutions, and queue managers), as well as functional capabilities. The system also provides operation planning, tracking patient status in the queue, recording interaction results, and generating reports for various levels of healthcare management.

internatura.description: The provided documents relate to the Ukrainian internship system for medical and pharmaceutical specialists.
internatura.commentary: The documents cover regulatory aspects, including training planning, enrollment, transfer, and expulsion of interns, as well as financial matters regarding budget and contract training. Additionally, the materials describe the functioning of the electronic rating distribution system, which ensures transparency of the admission campaign, graduate data management, application submission, vacancy review, and monitoring of the internship training process. The evaluation system and the rights and responsibilities of interns are also mentioned.

vacancies.description: The provided documents provide an overview of the Unified Web Portal of Vacant Positions in state and municipal healthcare institutions and the related Electronic Rating Distribution System for internship.
vacancies.commentary: The documents detail the purpose, functionality, and technical aspects of these systems, including user management, vacancy creation, application processing, and interaction with other registries. Installation, configuration, backup, and software recovery procedures are also discussed. Additionally, the sources describe user role models and legislative acts regulating the systems' operation, emphasizing their compliance with Ukrainian legislation and security standards.

bpr.description: The provided sources provide an overview of the continuous professional development (CPD) system.
bpr.commentary: The documents provide an overview of the electronic registration system for providers and continuous professional development (CPD) events for healthcare workers in Ukraine. The CPD system is a key tool for automating registration, monitoring, and management processes of educational activities aimed at maintaining and improving the professionalism of medical and pharmaceutical specialists.

ekrov.description: The provided sources provide an overview of the implementation of the blood donation information and communication system.
ekrov.commentary: The main purpose of creating the System is: "ensuring convenience of blood and blood component donation for donors, simplifying blood and blood component donation procedures and increasing donor loyalty to the blood system, blood supply control, monitoring and inventory planning in blood system entities (inventory management), as well as implementing a remote communication system with donors, which should contribute to increasing volumes and ensuring sustainable supply of blood and blood components."

sen-ikp.description: The provided sources provide user instructions for the Service Recipient Cabinet of the SEN IKP system.
sen-ikp.commentary: The strategic importance of the system lies in providing a transparent and effective mechanism for interaction between responsible persons, state market surveillance authorities, and state organizations performing toxicological control. SEN IKP is a single point for providing, accumulating, and processing mandatory information about cosmetic products placed on the Ukrainian market.
